titleStmt.title: 189. Memorandum From Michael Donley of the National Security Council Staff to the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs ( of the National Security Council Staff to the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Carlucci))

div.head: 189. Memorandum From Michael Donley of the National Security Council Staff to the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Carlucci)Source: Reagan Library, Michael Donley Files, Subject File, [Joint Chiefs of Staff] JCS Meeting with President 12/19/86. Top Secret. Sent for action.

list.head: SUBJECT
list.item: Minutes of JCS meeting with the President

div.p: The JCS met with the President on December 19th to discuss progress in implementing NSDD–250, and the worldwide exercise program.

Further background material on NSDD–250 has been provided for your reference. NSDD–250 (at Tab E)Attached but not printed. See Document 152. directed the JCS to evaluate the military implications of proposals to eliminate ballistic missiles by 1996. The final report is not expected until the end of January, but an Initial Progress Report (Tab D)Attached but not printed. See Document 182. was sent to the President December 5th. A summary of the Progress Report is at Tab C.Attached but not printed. See Document 174.

In their meeting with the President the JCS went beyond the Progress Report and discussed their preliminary conclusions. Dr. Keel approved the memo to SecDef and CJCS (at Tab B)Attached but not printed. See Document 187. in response to the meeting and our analysis of the Progress Report.

The meeting Minutes were compiled and cross-checked against three sources: Donley notes; Linhard notes; and Admiral Crowe’s script for the 12/19 briefing. While we recognize the awkwardness of your approving the Minutes of a meeting you did not attend, the National
Security Advisor usually approves such Minutes prior to their transmittal to the Diarist.
That you approve the meeting Minutes at Tab A for signature by the Executive Secretary. Carlucci approved the recommendation. The minutes are attached but not printed; see Document 183.
Bill Cockell and Lint Brooks concur.
